calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-22-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Roger Gross for Thomas Lucas.

The Court stated that its main concern is that a court appointed fiduciary should supplement his own pleadings as well as sign and verify the supplements. Mr. Gross reported that the notes came out just as Mr. Lucas left for vacation, that all of the addressed issues were within Mr. Gross's knowledge, that this was a very complicated case with multiple appearances, claim for fraud by the 1/2 brother of the decedent, that they knew he was adopted but were unable to prove it until Judge Wiss went to the family law department about it, that most of his time was spent dealing with the adoption issue, that there is uninhabitable property with protected tenants, that in regards to the statutory time there are time sheets attached and that the time was reduced. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Dec-17-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Roger Gross for Thomas Lucas. Katherine West appeared by telephone for and with Paul Willis (also appeared by telephone).

Mr. Willis explained that he is a self employed engineering consultant and the type of services he provides, that he works an average of twenty hours a week and that his work requires him to travel in and out of the country. Mr. Gross explained circumstances and asked the Court to keep in mind that Mr. Willis was not aware of the adoption, and that at the time it appeared that Mr. Willis was the sole Beneficiary. The Petition for Instructions was taken under submission.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Petition To Determine Distribution Entitlement
Off Calendar Jun-11-2013 Continued To Jul-09-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Roger Gross for Thomas Lucas.

The Court stated that its concern was that the investigation to review Court records for adoption may not reveal the adoption. Mr. Gross stated that there is no proof of the adoption and, that confidentiality of records refers to Minors adoptionas but that there is no language with regard to adoptions of Adults. The Court informed Counsel that adoption records are maintained by the Family Law Department in room 402. The Petition to Determine Entitlement and the Accounting Petition were continued to July 9, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.
